title: True / False / Not Given
instructions: Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.

questions.statement: Sensorineural hearing loss is more prevalent than conductive hearing loss.
The World Health Organization predicts that one-third of the world's population will experience hearing loss by 2050.
All forms of hearing loss can be successfully treated with hearing aids.
Researchers have conclusively demonstrated that hearing loss directly causes cardiovascular disease.
Asymmetric hearing loss may serve as an early indicator of future cardiovascular problems.
According to the Lancet commission, hearing loss accounts for approximately seven per cent of dementia cases.
The study conducted by Séverine Sabia's team found a stronger link between hearing loss and dementia than previous research had suggested.
One explanation for the association between hearing loss and dementia is that increased cognitive load may contribute to brain deterioration.
The phase III trial of the Australian gene therapy has already published its final results in a peer-reviewed journal.
The article states that hearing aids are equally effective for all categories of people at risk of dementia.
The article explains exactly how oxidative stress damages structures within the inner ear.
Researchers expect gene therapy to replace cochlear implants completely within the next decade.
